author: layla
body: Should we standardise the germination scoring window to 21 days across both sites?

author: amina
body: Agreed, 21 days works for the Nairobi lines too.

author: amina
body: Can we lock the workplan by end of June to de-risk the Oct 2 deadline?

author: layla
body: Yes. I'll circulate a near-final draft before next call.

author: rebecca
body: Please confirm the NNU overhead rate before the budget is finalised.

author: layla
body: Following up with you this week, Rebecca. Thanks.

author: tunde
body: Three EACRI lines germinated poorly here; logged in the field log.

author: layla
body: Noted. Let's discuss a pre-treatment step with Amina.

author: derek
body: Updated the cold-chain step after the March dry-spell handling issue.

author: layla
body: Good. Make sure Samuel and the field team see the change.

author: derek
body: Should variety be random or fixed in the yield model?

author: layla
body: Fixed for the comparison plots, random in the full model.

author: layla
body: Added the figure-format requirements from the WAJCR author guide.

author: grace
body: Seed access and processing cost come up far more than taste in the interviews.

author: layla
body: Let's fold that into the ESFES workshop content.

author: layla
body: Booklet draft v2 is ready; can you proof the Pidgin captions, Chidi?

author: derek
body: I'll cover the lab during your Ibadan trip in October.

author: layla
body: Thank you, Derek. I'll leave detailed handover notes.

author: amina
body: Happy to be co-author on the yam abstract.

author: layla
body: Great, I'll add you and send the draft for review.

author: samuel
body: Reminder: enter field data same-day before backing up to Drive.
